name: state
context: State management patterns: React state, context, Zustand, Jotai, Redux Toolkit, TanStack Query
info: State management is choosing the right tool for the right kind of state. Local component state uses useState/useReducer. Cross-component state uses React context or external stores like Zustand or Jotai. Server state (data fetched from APIs) uses TanStack Query for caching, deduplication, and background refetching. Redux Toolkit remains viable for large teams with complex shared state. This guide helps agents select the right pattern based on the state's scope, update frequency, and persistence needs. All patterns integrate with [[frontend/react]] components.
triggers: which state library should i use

sections.title: Local component state
sections.body: useState for simple values, useReducer for complex transitions. Keep state close to where it's used. Lift state up only when siblings share it. Derived values should be computed during render, not stored in state. See [[frontend/react#hooks]] for hook details.

sections.title: React context
sections.body: createContext + Provider for cross-cutting, infrequently-changing values: theme, auth, locale, feature flags. Wrap useContext in a custom hook. All consumers re-render on any context value change — avoid for high-frequency updates. See [[frontend/react#context]] for implementation patterns.

sections.title: Zustand
sections.body: Lightweight store with hook-based API. No providers needed. Selector-based subscriptions prevent unnecessary re-renders. Built-in middleware: persist (localStorage/sessionStorage), devtools (Redux DevTools), immer (immutable updates), subscribeWithSelector. Ideal for shared client state: cart, UI preferences, filters, navigation state.

sections.title: TanStack Query
sections.body: Server state management: fetching, caching, deduplication, background refetching, optimistic updates, infinite scroll. useQuery for reads, useMutation for writes, useInfiniteQuery for pagination. queryKey-based cache invalidation. staleTime controls cache freshness. Never use useEffect + useState for data fetching — use TanStack Query instead.

sections.title: Choosing the right tool
sections.body: Start with useState. Lift state when siblings share data. Use context for infrequent global values (theme, auth). Use Zustand for frequent shared client state. Use TanStack Query for all server/API state. Use Jotai for atomic bottom-up state. Use Redux Toolkit only if team already uses Redux. The wrong choice is not fatal — all patterns are interchangeable.

links.context: Testing components that use state stores
Server components access data directly, reducing client state

Zustand and Jotai are tree-shakeable, Redux Toolkit is heavier

related.note: State management is consumed by React components via hooks
